series_description: The Employer Disability Relief Program is used to encourage employers to employ and retain employees with disabilities as specified in Ohio R.C. 4123.343 by charging a portion of the claim to the State Surplus Fund, for lost time claims of their disabled employee rather than charging the employer’s risk fund for the entire amount of the claim. This packet contains the following documents: Application for Disability Relief – Form CHP-4A, Notice of Informal Conference for Employers, Notice of Informal Conference for Representatives, BWC Predetermination Worksheet, BWC Determination Worksheet, BWC Disability Relief Determination Order, Notices from Third Party Administrators of Withdrawal of an application First Report of an Injury, Occupational Disease or Death, Clinical Reports - General Correspondence, Medical Records. The Disability Relief Applications are submitted by employers and their Representatives to BWC requesting relief. The BWC Disability Relief Orders are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS) of the Employer Risk File, which are retained for the retention schedule of the Employer’s Risk File. See 855-0976 Employer Risk File Retention Schedule. The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file. The Disability Relief Application data and the BWC Determination Order data are entered into the Disability Relief Database. The Data is kept for the retention schedule of the Disability Relief Database. See 8551154 Disability Relief Database Retention Schedule. Finally, the complete application packet with BWC Determination Order are scanned into the injured workers claim file and retained for the retention schedule of the claim file. See 855-0265 Claim File Retention Schedule. BWC Determination Orders can be appealed to the Industrial Commission but are not litigated.
retention_statement: Electronic: The BWC Disability Relief Determination Orders are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS) of the Employer Risk File. Retain the electronic records for 10 years, and

series_description: The Compensation Reviews are utilized by Team Leaders to review all compensation paid by Claims Service Specialists for accuracy and appropriateness; at the correct rate, for the correct period of time, the correct type, (LM, TT, WL), if POA’s were honored, if the mailing address is correct, etc. (LM) Living Maintenance Compensation, (TT) Temporary Total Compensation, (WL) Wage Loss Compensation and (POA) Power Of Attorney. The goal of the Compensation Review is to conduct an audit to ensure the CSS’s are carefully creating all indemnity plans, regardless of the amount or payment period and to determine if a red flag exist. The Claim Audits are utilized by Team Leaders is to validate CSS/DMC/CSA1/OP2/Nurse compliance with policy, procedure, and different guidelines that have been identified as required in order to achieve statewide consistency, accuracy, and quality. Results identify areas where additional focus may be needed at any level – statewide, within a few offices, a specific office or CSS/DMC/CSA1/OP2/Nurse.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in house for 1 year

series_description: The Degree of Disability Management (DODM) model was developed by BWC in conjuction with Milliman & Robertson, a nationally renowned health care consultant. The DODM is a proactive disability strategy for returning injured workers to work. The DODM takes into account both the injury and the injured worker''s occupation and establishes an optimal "well managed" return to work date for each injury/occupation combination. For example, a construction worker who breaks a leg is expected to be off work longer than an office worker who breaks a leg, because of how the injury relates to his or her job. Likewise, the construction worker''s DODM optimal return-to-work date will follow a longer period of disability than that of the office worker.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain these records in office for 2 years ; Electronic: Retain these records in office for 2 years

Metrics may include, but are not limited to, tally of online and hardcopy pages dispersed to the units, levels of completed requests with promised due dates, metrics for environmental, electrical and other conditions The Statistical Reports are maintained electronically by the Infrastructure and Technology (IT) Department.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain the Statistical Reports for 3 years, and

series_description: The Immigration Form (I-9) verifies an individual eligibility to work in the United States. The form contains the name, address, two forms of identifications, copy of social security card, copy of drivers license and /or passport number of an employee from another country to prove citizenship status. Federal law requires that I-9 form be retained until 1 year after separation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 1 year after separation, and

series_description: The Overtime/Compensatory Time Request Form is completed by the BWC employee requesting to work overtime during the pay period. The form has the projected work hours, work accomplished, and if the employee would like to receive overtime or compensatory time. The form is signed by the employee, the supervisor, the director, and the division chief.
retention_statement: Paper: To be retained with the supervisor for one year, and

series_description: The Retrospective Rating Master File/UB230 is information used to balance payroll and premium for the Retrospective Rating Program. This information allows BWC to ensure that the employer is paying the correct premium. The primary information included in these reports includes changes in the experience modifier (EM), reported payroll and estimated payroll at application. The report also contains any changes in status for the employer such as transfers, combines, ect. This information contained in this file is also used to determine the adjusted premium for an employer who participates in the Retropective Rating Program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years

series_description: The Internal Web Pages for BWC are on the intranet and describe services provided by the BWC Departments and Units, available self service offerings on Division Pages, and team project sites (e.g. SharePoint) used for collaboration.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until updated, and

series_title: Internal Disability Management Files of BWC Employees.
series_description: The Internal Disability Management Files are claims filed by BWC employees who will be off work utilizing their Disability Leave Benefits. The records describe their medical condition/procedure, any forms and/or communication with DAS regarding the claim, and any related payroll records. The Internal Disability Management Files of BWC Employees are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Digitally image the Internal Disability Management Files. The scanned versions of the Internal Disability Management Files are to be verified for quality assurance. Retain 120 days after they have been imaged, and ; Electronic: The machine readable information will be reviewed every 3 years to ensure that the files can still be accessed. Retain the electronic files 7 years after employee separation from the BWC, and

series_description: The Directives, Manuals and Handbooks are publications produced by the BWC or the departments (e.g. Finance, Medical, Special Investigations etc) containing articles that may have confidential/sensitive information, operations, regulations, and procedures relating to the BWC.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced, and ; Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete, or replaced, and

series_description: Requests for Investigations are requests made for an outside agency (such as the State Highway Patrol, Auditor, etc.) to investigate a BWC employee. Included in the Request for Investigation are the actual request, referrals, preliminary investigation documents, etc.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain these records for 1 year after the completion of the investigation or decision not to investigate, and ; Electronic: Retain these records for 1 year after the completion of the investigation or decision not to investigate, and

series_description: The fraud attorney preparation files contain attorney work product used by an attorney representing the Special Investigation Unit (SIU) when the SIU is brought before the Industrial Commission. Attorney work product is any document or material prepared by an attorney, those working for the attorney, in anticipation of a lawsuit or other form of litigation.
retention_statement: Electronic: The fraud attorney preparation files are to be kept through the current pending fraud hearing and appeal on the claim in question; ; Paper: The fraud attorney preparation files are to be kept through the current pending fraud hearing and appeal on the claim in question;

series_description: The Closed Subrogation Cases records include files for subrogation cases that have been collected or closed as uncollectible. These files include correspondence, notes, pleadings, check copies, and settlement agreements. The Closed Subrogation Cases are scanned into a secure online repository. The secured online repository is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in a secure online repository, keep the subrogation case tracker for 10 years after the subrogation file has closed and ; Paper: Hold the records in the Subrogation Unit after case is closed. Scan into a secure online repository

series_title: Retrospective Rating Plan Annual Loss Statement (Retro Annual Report)
series_description: This record is an annual evaluation statement for the retrospective rating plan. This report is run at the end of each policy year. The retro annual report posts data to let employers and TPA''s (third party administrators) know what the employer is being charged. Since BWC bills employers annually, this statement/report serves as their annual bill for the program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years provided audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Electronic: Retain 2 years provided audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved ; Microform: Retain 2 years provided audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved
disposition: Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Delete ; Microform: Shred

series_description: The Training Files consist of applications for training, seminars and workshops (both internal & external). It list the course requested, the persons name that is requesting the course and it can also be used to confirm the students attendance in a class.
retention_statement: Paper: Records must be retained for 7 years per IACET, the accrediting CEU (Continuing Education Unit) organization used at OCOSH. ; Electronic: Records must be retained for 7 years per IACET, the accrediting CEU (Continuing Education Unit) organization used at OCOSH.

series_title: Safety Council New Enrollments, Deletions and Policy Number Updates
series_description: The safety council new enrollments, deletions and policy number updates consist of BWC policy numbers, number of employees, company name and location along with signatures of the person completing the enrollment form. This series is compiled by the safety council to enroll, delete and update policy numbers received by members to be considered members of one of the safety councils located around the state of Ohio.
retention_statement: Paper: If scanned securely shred, if not scanned retain for 2 years ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years

series_title: Safety Council Evaluations and Sign-in Sheets
series_description: The Safety Council evaluations and sign-in sheets consist of BWC policy numbers, member and representative name and signatures. This series is compiled by the Safety Council to determine the eligibility of a member for the Safety Council Rebate program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years

series_description: The Mock File Inventory spreadsheet is prepared by the Staff Counsel for organization of litigation and preparation of litigation strategy. The spreadsheet documents various information concerning mock files. The document is continually updated, usually once every two to four weeks.
retention_statement: Electronic: Four weeks and

series_title: Attorney General’s Office Litigation Summary Worksheet
series_description: The Attorney General’s Office Litigation Summary Worksheet is prepared by the Legal Counsel and used to document monetary authority given to the Attorney General’s Office to settle a particular court case. The document lists the court case number, the claim number, the AAG assigned to the case, the BWC attorney who reviewed the claim file and the amount authorized for settlement by the BWC attorney.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain one year and

series_description: The Court Docket Worksheet is prepared by the Legal staff and used internally by the units to construct a docket of court appearances and mediation appearances, as requested by the Ohio Attorney General’s Office. This is an ongoing document and is updated approximately on a weekly basis.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain one week.

series_description: The Hospital Reimbursement Review Bill Worksheets contain the injured worker claim numbers and billing information; which are populated from a data warehouse report. This worksheet is simply used to help bill reviewers do research faster than looking up the information in the BWC system. The reviewer may make notes on the worksheet with additional research findings.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the Hospital Reimbursement Review Bill Worksheets for 2 years, provided audited by the Auditor of State, audit report is release, and

series_title: Audit Report on Cambridge Adjustments; Fee Entry
series_description: The Audit Report on Cambridge Adjustments; Fee Entry is the supervisor’s weekly audit of subordinate work for Cambridge Adjustments, suspense worked in Medical Invoice Information System (MIIS), and for fee bill entry in Medical Invoice Information System (MIIS) or Cambridge. The Cambridge Adjustments are adjustment requests submitted by the Managed Care Organizations (MCO) to adjust previously paid invoices in the Cambridge billing system.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office for 3 years or until internal audit review is completed ; Electronic: Retain in office for 3 years or until internal audit review is completed

series_description: The Appeals and Continuances Spreadsheet is prepared by Legal Counsel to assist in organization and prioritization of litigation, particularly BWC motions and appeals pending in the Industrial Commission. The spreadsheet is updated quarterly.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 90 days.

series_title: Medical Payment Dispute Committee (MPDC) Official Files.
series_description: These files contain the information submitted to the Medical Payment Dispute Committee (MPDC) for a conference. The MPDC is composed of representatives from the Legal Division, Medical Billing and Adjustments, Medical Policy and Support services. The files include copies of provider billing information concerning the provider’s dispute of the amount of overpayment made by the Bureau to the provider, a copy of Special Investigation Units /Report of Investigation, provider peer review of patient medical treatment notes with the appropriate medical billing code that should have been billed for services, spreadsheets of the provider overpayment, any evidence submitted at the Conference, any notes taken by the MCO Unit and the Official BWC MPDC Committee Order (Decision of the Committee).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain hard file in office for 2 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation. after 3 years move microfilm to offsite storage and store for 12 years. ; Electronic: Retain hard file in office for 2 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation. Retain CD’s or videos in office for 5 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation. After 5 years the move the CD’s or videos with the microfilm to offsite storage and store for 12 years. ; Microform: Retain hard file in office for 2 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation.
disposition: Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Burn ; Microform: Burn

series_title: Medical Payment Dispute Settlements Attorney Files
series_description: The Medical Payment Dispute Settlements Attorney Files contains attorney work product in representing the Special Investigation Department in negotiation of a Provider Overpayment Settlement. Included in the files are a copy of the Special Investigation Units’ Report of Investigation, Fraud Management System (FMS) notes, spreadsheets of the provider overpayment, medical reviews, copies of claim file information and a copy of the signed negotiated Settlement Agreement, copies of any checks received from providers for overpayment reimbursement by the Legal Division that were subsequently given to BWC Cash Control Unit for processing. These files were originally in preparation for a Conference before the Medical Payment Dispute Committee, but were settled before being heard by the Committee.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 15 years plus the life of the Settlement Agreement. Store at offsite storage ; Electronic: Retain for 15 years plus the life of the Settlement Agreement. Store at offsite storage

series_description: The Media Correspondence is a compilation of media advisories releases, and news outlet reports relating to BWC business. The Media Correspondence documents are created via the computer, list the dates of the press clips and media advisories. The Communications Division is to redact any confidential information in press releases and media advisories prior to being sent to the media. These records may be of historical value. Any information of historical value will be printed on paper and sent to the State Archives at the Ohio Historical Society within two years of creation.
retention_statement: Paper: Provide printed copies of Media Correspondence that is of historical value to the State Archives. ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years, and

series_title: C 17 – Outpatient Medication Reimbursement Request Form
series_description: The C 17 – Outpatient Medication Reimbursement Request Form is completed by the Pharmacy that dispensed the medication and is submitted by the injured worker to the Pharmacy Benefits Manager contracted by BWC. Injured workers only use this form for reimbursement of outpatient medication. The C 17 is scanned into the injured worker’s claim file and is to be retained for the retention period of the claim file.
retention_statement: Paper: Digitally image into the claim files, hold original documents 120 days for quality control, send to Mafil, and ; Electronic: Retain electronic images of claim files/documentation for the life of the claim plus 20 years, and

series_title: Rehabilitation Services Commission (RSC) Monthly Management File Transfer
series_description: The Rehabilitation Services Commission (RSC) Monthly Management File contains a report of RSC’s key performance metrics. Data allows BWC to determine what our dollar contribution is, return on investment.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain the RSC Monthly Management File Transfer, on the I: Drive for 15 years in the Rehab Policy folder, and

series_title: Rehabilitation Services Commission (RSC) Weekly Eligibility File Transfer
series_description: The Rehabilitation Services Commission (RSC) Weekly Eligibility File Transfer contains an exchange of data between the RSC and the BWC to verify an individual’s eligibility for funding through the cooperative agreement.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain the RSC Monthly Management File Transfer, on the I: Drive for 15 years in the Rehab Policy folder, and

series_description: The BWC Board of Directors Correspondence includes correspondence (electronic form letters) from the public, companies, and organizations concerning issues. The form letters from the public may state an opinion for or against a particular vote. The general correspondence (electronic form letters) from the Board of Directors includes responses to inquiries and other communication with the public.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 1 year in the I drive in the Board of Directors folder, and

series_description: The ETA Tracking System stores employer stats, including past history of activity that BWC Employer Specialists, Business Consultants and our Safety Consultants provided, regarding the amount of time spent and what was discussed with each customer. The Tracking System was created in 1999 however after 2004 the Tracking System became a query only system due to no information being stored into the Tracking System.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until the employer is no longer in existance ; Electronic: Retain until the employer is no longer in existance

series_description: The Subpoena Request for Records is used in the discovery of court cases.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for six (6) months in the Legal Division, and ; Electronic: Retain for six (6) months in the Legal Division, and

series_title: Microfilm of Cashed Warrants (Rolls of Film)
series_description: The rolls of film contain copies (front and back) of checks cashed by the claimants, medical providers, and attorneys.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain 10 years

series_description: The Printing Request form (ADM 0515) is the returned copy from the Department of Administrative Services (DAS), which is used to order printed material.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain one fiscal year, and

series_description: The microfiche is used to obtain copies of warrants that were cashed by the claimants, medical providers, and attorneys.

series_description: The Client Health Records includes all original treatment documentation on clients who have been through a rehabilitation program or evaluation at the J. Leonard Camera Center or the Walker Center.
retention_statement: Microform: Transfer the original microfilm to an offsite records facility. Retain the original microfilm for 10 years. Copies of the microfilm are to be retained in office for 10 years. ; Paper: Retain in office 3 years after treatment is complete

retention_statement: Microform: Retain for 10 years, and ; Paper: Retain until filmed, checked for quality assurance, and

series_title: Microfiche Listing of Cash Cancelled Warrants
series_description: The Microfiche Listing of Cash Cancelled Warrants contains information as to whether warrants have been cashed or cancelled by the injured workers.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain the Microfiche Listing of Cash Cancelled Warrants in the Microfilm Unit for 10 years, and

series_description: The Lists and Directories includes mailing lists, directories, rosters, and registers complied by agency.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced, and ; Paper: Retain until superseded, obsolete or replaced, and

series_description: The Executive Orders and Proclamations are instructions issued by the Governor as the Chief Executive of State Government (Original on file permanently with Secretary of State).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year, and ; Electronic: Retain in the BWC Administrator’s email system for 1 year, and

series_description: The Medical Provider Complaint Files contain complaints and correspondence concerning the medical providers. The Medical Provider Complaint Files are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images. The Disability Evaluators Panel (DEP) recruit, credential, and maintain the DEP physician network that provide independent medical exams and medical file reviews for BWC statewide. The DEP provides resolutions on all DEP inquiries including billing and exam complaints.
retention_statement: Electronic: The Medical Provider Complaint Files are located on the DEP SharePoint site/DEP Complaint Tracker in an Excel spreadsheet. Retain until the provider is terminated from Disability Evaluators Panel (DEP) plus an additional 7 years. The Medical Provider Complaint Files are scanned and maintained in the UDS Doctor File. Retain the Provider Complaint Files in UDS until the provider is terminated from the Disability Evaluators Panel (DEP) plus an additional 7 years. The machine readable information will be reviewed every 3 years to ensure the Medical Provider Complaint Files can still be accessed. ; Paper: Digitally image the Medical Provider Complaint Files. After the scanned versions of the Medical Provider Complaint Files have been verified for quality assurance, send to Mafil, and

series_title: Files from Closed Managed Care Organizations (MCO)
series_description: The Files from Closed Managed Care Organizations (MCO) list the MCO’s that are no longer in business. The records contain work papers, MCO Provider account banking statements and reconciliation documents, General Accounting Deposit records, cancelled checks, bill payments, and on-site audit reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the files from the Managed Care Organizations (MCO) that are no longer in business, in the office for 3 years

series_title: Managed Care Organizations (MCO) Appeal Response Letters
series_description: The Managed Care Organizations (MCO) Appeal Response Letters are correspondence (concerning the MCO audit, which was preformed by BWC) sent from the MCO to the BWC. The MCO Appeal Response Letters contain the drafts of audit reports, MCO appeals (concerning the lack of controls discovered during the audits), and the final MCO audit reports.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the MCO Appeal Response Letters, in the office for 3 years

series_description: The AC-3 is a Temporary Authorization form submitted by the Third Party Administrator (TPA). The AC-3 gives the TPA temporary authorization to review and work on certain BWC matters on the employer’s behalf. The AC-3 is date stamped then scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS) to retained for reviewing purposes by BWC if needed. The AC-3 is good for only nine months after it has been date stamped by BWC. After the nine month period is reached, the TPA is unable to review or work on any BWC matters for that employer.
retention_statement: Paper: The AC-3 forms are to be retained for one week after they are scanned into the Universal Document Service for quality assurance ; Electronic: The AC-3 forms are to be retained in the Universal Document Service (UDS) for one year

series_title: Dual Public Employment Form for R.C. 102.04(D) Statement
series_description: The R.C. 102.04(D) Statement form is required to be completed by public employees who are employed by more than one public employer at the same time. This information is required by the R.C. 102.04(D).
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain the electronic records in the Legal Division’s I drive for 4 years, and ; Paper: Scan the paper documents, verify for quality assurance, and

series_description: All BWC Departments send a copy of the issued Return Gifts and Gratuities Letter to the General Legal Counsel. The Office of General Counsel will retain the letter in a shared drive
retention_statement: Electronic: Electronic: Retain the issued letter for 1 year, and ; Paper: Paper: Scan the paper documents, verify for quality assurance, retain the paper documents for 30 days, and

series_title: Adjudicating Committee and Administrator’s Designee’s Internal Correspondence
series_description: The Adjudicating Committee and Administrator’s Designee’s Internal Correspondence is created by the Adjudicating Committee members in deliberation on a committee decision and /or any of these members correspondence or The Administrator’s Designee’s Correspondence (On second level hearings) with the Adjudicating Committee Secretary prior to issuing of an Adjudicating Order.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years in the E-mail repository

series_description: The Adjudication Committee Hearing Packets are created by the Adjudicating Unit in the Legal Division. The Adjudication Committee Hearing Packets are for the Adjudication Committee Members / Administrator's Designee / Legal Counsel to the Administrator's Designee that are scheduled to preside over the hearing for which the packet was created. The hearing packets contain the employer’s formal complaint, the Bureau’s position on the issue, and/or the Employer Group’s formal complaint, statement of facts, complaint decision, compliant denial letter, and any cases, rules, or laws being relied upon by the parties. These records are created digitally and maintained in the Bureau's document retention system..
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years from the date of hearings, and

series_description: The Adjudication Assignment Tracking Schedule is used to assign qualified staff in the Employer Management Services department to regularly scheduled adjudication hearings. Assignment is done on a rotation basis and a schedule is needed in order to keep assignments given in a fair and consistent manner.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years

series_description: This record is used for documenting the arrival and departure times of BWC employees and visitors that come into a BWC office building during and/or after normal business hours.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain these records in office for 1 year ; Electronic: Retain these records for 1 year

series_description: The Assessment Scores record indicates the test results of individual questions given on a test. The Assessment Scores are used to identify areas in which students have difficulty and need to improve overall quality of the course.
retention_statement: Electronic: The Information is to be retained on the BWC (LMS) Learning Management System until the BWC employee no longer an active employee with BWC. ; Paper: Once the record is scanned into the (LMS) Learning Management System, hold for 30 days for quality assurance

series_description: General Correspondence includes internal and external correspondence (letters, memos) from individuals, companies, and organizations requesting information pertaining to BWC, legal interpretations, and other miscellaneous inquiries. The general correspondence is informative (it does not attempt to influence BWC policy).
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 1 year, and ; Electronic: Retain 1 year, and

series_description: This file consist of the doctors evalution of findings report and a brief description of the problem which has caused the deptartment to schedule the employee fitness evaluation. the file contains but is not limited to the employees fitness for duty, physical information,psychological information, advise as to whether or not the employee should recieve a copy of the report, if the employee is a threat to himself or others,disability application, letter to noitify employee of examination date, return to work letter,employee history screen, any correspondence between management used to determine if an evaluation is necessary, letter to notify employee of a meeting to discuss disability separation.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain record for the duration of employee''s employment

series_description: Audit reports and work papers created by an agency''s internal audit unit or similar functioning audit.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years after department is audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released ; Electronic: Retain for 5 years

series_title: Confidential Personal Information (CPI) System Access Logs
series_description: The Confidential Personal (CPI) System Access Logs keeps track of who has accessed confidential personal information (CPI) pursuant to
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain the CPI System Access Logs for a rolling 60 month (5 years) basis. When a record reaches the 61st month from the time it was entered into the log, delete it from the log file.

series_description: The Subrogation Escrow Account Files are for accounts that are still being collected based on an escrow agreement including copies of checks for amount collected to date, collection correspondence, requests to bill, billing records, and subrogation settlement agreements. The Subrogation Escrow Account Files are scanned into a secure online repository. The secured online repository is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 10 years after the subrogation file has closed, and ; Paper: Retain after the account is collected

series_title: Release of Salvage Equipment or Property Files
series_description: The Release of Salvage Equipment or Property Files record the salvage of equipment or property belonging to the State of Ohio. The Release of Salvage Equipment or Property Files include the Surplus Turn In Document ADM-3672 form that is signed by the appropriate personnel at the Department of Administrative Services.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the Release of Salvage Equipment or Property Files 4 years in the Inventory Control Unit, provided audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved, and

series_description: The Check Balancing Logs are completed for each check run. The Check Balancing Logs contain the number of checks used; the number range of the checks, the dollar value of checks printed, balances, etc. The Check Balancing Logs are completed by computer operators and are signed off by the shift supervisor.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years provided until audited by Auditor of State, audit report is released, all discrepancies are resolved, and ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years in the Operations I: Drive folder, provided until audited by Auditor of State, audit report is released, all discrepancies are resolved, and

series_description: Electronic records of employee's name and (PN) position number, on payroll, their classification, hourly rate of pay, civil service status, total payroll cost and total benefits cost.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in office 3 years

series_description: The Building Maintenance Manuals indicate the maintenance requirement for individual pieces of equipment.
retention_statement: Paper: The Building Maintenance Manuals are to be retained in Facilities Management until update is received or equipment is salvaged, and

series_description: The Project Specifications & Building Plans (Building construction documents, drawings and specification. Including but not limited to, foundation architectural mechanical. Electrical, finishes, life safety, date /phone plumbing, structural etc. specification to include hardware mechanical systems, closeout documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in Facilities Management until BWC no longer occipies the tenant space in buidling or lease termination. ; Electronic: Retain in Facilities Management until BWC no longer occipies the tenant space in buidling or lease termination

series_description: The Software Licenses are for software purchased by the Information and Technology Division which state how many users are permitted to use the software.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until licenses become obsolete, software is no longer used, replaced with updated or new licenses, and

series_description: The Inquires and Complaints are made by telephone, computer, letter or in person; as well as the disposition of such inquiries.
retention_statement: Paper: Hard copy to be retained 4 years in the Ombuds office, and ; Electronic: Electronic records are to be retained 4 years in the Ombuds email repository, and

series_description: The Records Retention Schedules are created by the Legal Division’s RIM Unit and used to identify the length of time a record must be retained before final disposition. The Retention Schedules are for the sections of BWC and have been approved for use.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the work product and schedule until superseded by a revised retention schedule, or until record series is no longer maintained, and ; Electronic: Retain the work product and schedule until superseded by a revised retention schedule, or until record series is no longer maintained, and

series_title: Group Rating Safety Plan Summary Form (SH2)
series_description: The Group Rating Safety Plan and supportive documentation are received annually from Group Rating and Group Retrospective Rating Sponsors to document how they have met safety requirements of 4123.17.68. The purpose of this rule is to establish minimum safety requirements for group experience and group retrospective rating. The forms are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years according to ORC 4123.24 which states the records shall be preserved for at least five years after the respective times of the transactions upon which the records are based. ; Electronic: Retain in UDS for 5 years according to ORC 4123.24 which states the records shall be preserved for at least five years after the respective times of the transactions upon which the records are based.

series_description: The Group Applications (AC-26) are received by BWC from the Third Party Administrators (TPA). The Group Applications documents the employer’s intent to participate in the Group Rating Program with BWC. When an employer is accepted into the Group Rating program that employer receives a discount on their BWC rates. The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain these records in the Group Rating unit for 5 years. After the 5 years has passed, send the Group Applications to storage for an additional 5 years, and ; Electronic: If not stored in the employer’s file, save electronically in a shared drive for 10 years, and

series_title: Retrospective Rating Program Material and Application Information
series_description: The Retrospective Rating Program Material and Application Information includes: the application for the program, initial minimum premium percentage worksheets, eligibility report, correspondence, financial evaluation worksheet, letter of acceptance or rejection for the program, annual evaluation and adjustments, minimum premium percentage worksheets, claim/payroll adjustments and GAAP audited financial statements. If the information the employer filled out on these documents meets the criteria for the Retrospective Rating Program then they may participate in the program. The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 12 years if documents are not scanned into UDS. If scanned into UDS, destroyed after it has been verified that a readable copy has been stored electronically, and ; Electronic: Retain in UDS for 12 years, and

series_title: Managed Care Organization (MCO) Report Cards and Supporting Documentation
series_description: The Managed Care Organization Report (MCO) Cards contains MCO performance evaluations and compares results of injured worker and employer surveys for later use for employers during their selection of an MCO.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain these records for 2 years ; Electronic: Retain on the BWC server for 17 years, and

series_title: API reports for Subsequent 148''s and MCO notes
series_description: The Application Processing Interface (API) reports for Subsequent 148''s and MCO notes provide a breakdown of the performance and production of the Managed Care Organization (MCO). This information is sent from the BWC Information Technology (IT) Department to the BWC MCO Business Unit for review.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain these records for 2 years ; Electronic: Retain on the BWC server for 17 years, and

series_title: Managed Care Organization (MCO) Contract Development Papers
series_description: The Managed Care Organization (MCO) Contract Development Papers contains information about the development and analysis of the proposed contract changes made by the BWC Managed Care Organization (MCO) Business & Reporting Unit.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 2 years after the end of the affiliated contract, and ; Electronic: Retain for 2 years after the end of the affiliated contract, and

series_description: Applications for Family and Medical Leave (Records) and any information related to use of the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) including: Basic payroll and identifying employee data, Dates/hours FMLA leave is taken by eligible employees, Notices of leave furnished by employee under FMLA, Notices given to employees as required by FMLA regulations, Descriptions of employee benefits and paid/unpaid leave polices and practices. Premium payments of employee benefits, Records of any dispute between employer and employee regarding designation of leave as FMLA leave.
retention_statement: Paper: Hard copy FMLA records from the period of 1993 to 1999 should be retained for 7 years after the employee’s separation from BWC according to the general schedule 18550857. All records from 2000 and beyond will be scanned into the FMLA SharePoint site and ; Electronic: Retain for 3 calendar years after the employee’s separation from BWC. This retention period is in according The Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993 under the Federal code 29 CFR 825.400

series_description: The Mock Files are appeals made by attorneys to a higher hearing level regarding a workers’ compensation issue. The mock files are maintained by the Legal Division for legal appeals with the Industrial Commission.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 90 days after the last order where no further appeal is filed, and ; Electronic: Retain the emails for 90 days after the last order where no further appeal is filed, and

series_description: Part of the Civil Service Application - A perforated attachment that is to be separated from the Civil Service Application when submitted. This record is a request for biographical data about the applicant.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office area for 1 week after scanned into (UDS) Universal Document Service for quality purposes ; Electronic: Retain in (UDS) Universal Document Service for 2 years from the date of entry

series_description: The Family Support Orders, which are not associated with any claims, are sent into BWC from the 88 Child Support Agencies in Ohio and from other Child Support Agencies throughout the United States. The Family Support Orders are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images. The scanned documents of the Family Support Orders are attached to the appropriate claims.
retention_statement: Electronic: The machine readable information will be reviewed every 3 years to ensure that the files can still be accessed. Retain the electronic images of the Family Support Orders for 20 years, and ; Paper: Scan the original documents of the Family Support Orders into UDS, retain the original documents in office for 120 days for quality assurance, and

series_description: The Telephone/Cell Phone Invoices list incoming and outgoing calls made by the BWC employees and the costs of the incoming and outgoing calls during a particular time period. The Telephone/Cell Phone Invoices are distributed to the departments each month, and then the invoices are passed out to the BWC employees for their signatures and are kept in the departments.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 2 years provided audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved, and ; Electronic: Retain in office 2 years provided audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved, and

series_description: This record is created when there has been an overlapping period of unemployment compensation, temporary total (TT) and living maintainance (LM) workers compensation payments made during the same period. Upon indentification of a matching period, an email is created notifying the assigned claims service specialist (CSS) who will then review for any overpayments.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 1 year ; Electronic: Retain for 1 year

series_description: The Class Rosters for Outside Businesses contains the student’s name, the name of the student’s company, the address of the student’s company, and the name of the class being taken by the student at the BWC. The Class Rosters are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain in UDS for 10 years, and ; Paper: Retain for 5 years, and

series_description: The Analytical Trending and Forecasting Reports are ad hoc reports created in response to specific requests for business information. Reports are used to assist in decision making and may contain long or short term trending or forecasting requiring years worth of information. Examples include, but are not limited to; trending and forecasting of diagnosis, medical procedures, duration of disability, compensation payments, premium receipts, injury and policy demographics, and geographical profiles. These analyses require the ability to retain data that is no longer needed for ongoing operational purposes.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until there is no longer an operational need, and

series_description: Transient Documents includes telephone messages, post-it-notes, drafts, Microsoft Outlook, emails, and other limited documents which convey information of temporary importance in lieu of oral communication.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until no longer of administrative value, and ; Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value, and

series_description: The Deceased Injured Worker (here after known as DIW) Claim Files contains documents related to a claim for workers’ compensation benefits due to a work-related injury resulting in the death of an employee. These Claim files may include (but are not limited to); Dependent death benefits, all correspondence, BWC Orders, IC Orders, Family Support Orders, Forms: FROI-1 the First Report of Injury, C-101 Authorization to Release Medical Information, C-86 Motion of Claimant, IC Hearing Documents, C-92 Percentage Applications and Awards, medical documents, Medical Peer Reviews, Medical and non-medical Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) packets, x-ray interpretations, psychological reports, C-84 Request for Temporary Total Compensation, Permanent Total Disability Compensation Application, Vocational Rehabilitation Reports, Wage Loss Application (working and non-working wage loss), Living Maintenance Compensation Application, Complete Settlement Packets and Settlement Agreement, Disability Relief and Disability Relief Award Orders. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after with DOI on or after 9/15/2020 on a Medical Only Claim, the claim expires five (5) years from the date of the last medical service rendered, compensation payment, or date of death. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 08/25/2006 to 9/14/2020 on a Medical Only Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 12/11/1967 to 8/24/2006 on a Medical Only Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims for Date of Injury Prior to 12/11/1967 on a Medical Only Claim: Retain ten (10) years from last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or ten (10) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after with DOI on or after 9/15/2020 on a Lost Time Claim, the claim expires five (5) years from the date of the last medical service rendered, compensation payment, or date of death. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 08/25/2006 to 9/14/2020 on a Lost Time Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, or date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 12/11/1967 to 8/24/2006 on a Lost Time Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims for Date of Injury Prior to 12/11/1967 on a Lost Time Claim: Retain ten (10) years from last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or ten (10) years from the date of injury if no medical payments.
retention_statement: Microform: Digitally image the claim mail into the claim file, verify a clear copy and ; Electronic: Digitally image into the appropriate claims file, hold according to the appropriate statutory time frame (listed in the description above); and ; Paper: Digitally image into the appropriate claims file, hold according to the appropriate statutory time frame (listed in the description above); and
disposition: Microform: Burn ; Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred

series_description: The BWC conducts audits of self insuring employers to ascertain compliance with Chapters 4121 and 4123 of the Ohio Revised and Administrative Codes along with BWC policies and procedures. The Compliance and Paid Compensation Audits contain reports, related working papers, supporting documents and orientations included. The Compliance and Paid Compensation Audits are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan the original document into UDS, verify the scanned image is readable, and ; Electronic: Retain in UDS for 6 years, and

series_description: This file is a record of the measures taken to prove/disprove an employees misconduct which may lead to discipline or any non-compliance by management of a bargaining contract. It must include the original grievance form (including the postmarked mailing envelope), the discipline notification letter and any documentation in support of the discipline or non-compliance. It will also include documentation from all of the grievance steps including documentation submitted by the grievant or union to refute the claim.
retention_statement: Electronic: Record is scanned into Employee and Labor Relations database and retained until employee separates from BWC or is no longer of administrative value. ; Paper: Retain in Personnel file according to collective bargaining agreement requirements

series_description: The SI employer has to file a Renewal Application (SI7) each year to renew their self insurance. The employer provides the BWC Self Insured Department with the most current information such as name, addresses, subsidiaries, locations, and parent information, including financial information. From the information the employer provided, the underwriting department will update the records to reflect the most current information. The Self Insured (SI) Employer Renewal Application is scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan the original document into UDS, verify the scanned image is readable, and ; Electronic: Retain for 10 years after the employer has gone out of business, and

series_title: Denied Self Insured Identification Number (SIDN) Application File
series_description: The Denied Self Insured Identification Number (SIDN) Application File is an application that is denied self insurance, and the employer is given 14 days to appeal to the Self Insured Review Panel (SIRP). The Self Insured Identification Number (SIDN) Application File is scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 1 year, and ; Paper: Scan the original document into UDS, verify the scanned image is readable, and

series_description: PDP was a discount program that was in effect from 1995 to 2009. It offered 5% and 10% discounts for different scheduled years of participation in the program. The UA-5 application was used as an enrollment form to participate in the PDP program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for one year ; Electronic: Retain for one year

series_description: The Physician Verification Slip is a record verifying the employee was seen by the physician. Employees submit a verification slip to the supervisor to meet requirements of a physician verification direct order.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 30 days per the BWC Employee Handbook rule ; Electronic: Retain for 30 days per the BWC Employee Handbook rule

series_description: PEO’s enter into an agreement with one or more client employers for the purpose of co-employing all or part of the client employer’s workforce at the client employer’s work site. The PEO file contains a Professional Employer Organization Client Relationship Notification (UA-3) form that is required for BWC to recognize a PEO relationship. The PEO file also contains the UA-3 form which specifically consists of new, change, and termination activity of the PEO. The UA-3 forms as well as the AC18 and AC19 forms contain claims and payroll experience data that the PEO Unit requires from the PEO community in order to transfer back to client policies upon changing or terminated PEO relationships. These records are the PEO’s active and inactive client base. The PEO files may include copies of random electronic correspondence and communications used to document PEO relationships as well.
retention_statement: Paper: After being scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS), retain for 30 days for quality assurance ; Electronic: Retain for 10 years

series_description: Copies of resolutions whch are generated by both BWC and IC. Used weekly as a point of reference in job function.
retention_statement: Paper: To be retained in Risk Management for twenty (20) years

series_description: The Disability Relief database is an access database that enables processing of Employer Disability Relief Reimbursement Applications. The Disability Relief database contains: the date applications are received, conferences docketed, a copy of the unsigned Determination Order, the date orders were issued and published, Notice of Informal Conference for Employers, Notice of Informal Conference for Representatives and Disability Relief special reports used by the Disability Relief Unit created from the raw data. The Disability Relief special reports used by the Disability Relief Unit are the Orders to Date issued 3 days of Determination Date yearly, Orders to date issued 15 days of Determination Date yearly, Orders to Date issued w/in 90 days of Receipt yearly, Over 90 days old aging report, Applications to Date-Aging, Average award Percentage, Attorneys average award percentage, Attorneys average award percentage by disability, Orders to Date issued w/in 180 days of Receipt Yearly, Orders to Date Issued w/in 365 Days of Receipt Yearly, Orders to Date w/Waivers Yearly, Orders to Date Yearly, Applications to Date- Monthly/Yearly, Rep Average Award Percentage, Disability Relief Hearing Report, Granted Detail List and Denial/Dismissal Detail List.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain Indefinitely

series_description: When an employee is imposed with discipline (i.e., working suspension, fine, non-working discipline) a Personnel Action (PA) form is created. The PA form allows Personnel to process the imposed discipline into OAK’s for payroll and work history purposes. The PA form is filed in the employee’s Personnel file for one year from the date of discipline is imposed.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until discipline becomes inactive

series_description: The Computer Usage Files contain electronic files, automated logs and web logs; created to monitor computer system usage by internal and external users. The Computer Usage Files may include (but are not limited to) log-in files, system usage files, data entry logs, records of individual computer program usage, websites usage and web page activity.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain for 1 year, and

series_description: These files contain applications, billings, payroll reports, penalty letters, worksheets, etc. for active risk.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain film for 10 years ; Paper: Retain in office for 5 years

series_description: The Departmental Policies and Procedures are the rules and regulations that are created by the BWC Administration or the Departments (e.g. Finance, Human Resources, Medical Services, Special Investigations, Operational Policy, and Vocational Rehabilitation) that may have confidential/sensitive information for employees to follow while being employed at BWC.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain these records for five years, and ; Paper: Retain these records for five years, and

series_description: The Self Insured Complaints – SI28 forms are filed against a self-insured employer. The Self Insured Complaints are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan the original document into UDS, verify the scanned image is readable, and ; Electronic: Retain 6 years, and

series_description: These are obtained from bankrupt/non-complying employers.
retention_statement: Paper: Files held in Self-Insured for 120 days.

series_description: Fiscal vouchers documenting the repayment of money to state insurance fund as a result of action of courts, Attorney General's office and Administrative Hearings.
retention_statement: Paper: After each case is completed, retain 1 year

series_title: Supporting Data of Base Rates and Merit Rate Rating for Private Employer Rate Calculation
series_description: This information is used to calculate rates for Private Employers (PA) including payroll, losses, expected loss rates, limited loss ratios, and experience modifiers.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the Actuarial Unit for 25 years, and ; Microform: Retain Microfiche in the Actuarial Unit for 25 years, and ; Electronic: Retain electronic records for 25 years on the (WCIS) Workers Comp Insurance System, Rates and Payments and BWC shared drives, and
disposition: Paper: Shred ; Microform: Burn ; Electronic: Delete

series_description: Rate reports and write-ups used to calculate premium rates for Private Employers (PA), Public Employer Taxing Districts (PEC), and Self-Insured Assessment Rates. These reports are created by the actuarial section underwriters documenting the rate process review and the detailed information for the rating process.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain the machine readable versions of the Actuarial Rate Reports for 25 years. ; Paper: Retain the Actuarial Rate Reports in the Actuarial Unit for 25 years.

series_title: Public Employer State Agency Rates (PES) Reports
series_description: These records are rate reports and write ups that support the annual calculation of rates for Public Employer State Agency Rates (PES). Included in the reports are payroll and premium information, individual claim costs, and statistical tabulations. "Write ups" is the actual terminology used by the Actuarial Department.
retention_statement: Microform: Retain the microfiche in the Actuarial Unit for 11 years, and ; Electronic: The electronic information is contained in access databases and excel spreadsheets saved on the I drive for 25 years, and ; Paper: Retain in the Actuarial Unit for 11 years, and
disposition: Microform: Burn ; Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred

series_description: Annual reports from the consulting firms hired as consultants by the Ohio Bureau of Workers’ Compensation Board of Directors. This report is a PDF file that is sent from the consulting firms and uploaded to Actuarial Unit’s SharePoint site.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in the Actuarial Unit for 25 years, and ; Electronic: Retain the Portable Document Format (PDF) file of annual reports permanent.

series_description: These testing forms and demographic sheets contain proficiency tests and results of prospective employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the completed Applicant test for 2 years. ; Electronic: Retain in the Access Database for 2 years, and

series_title: PERRP Occupational Health Enforcement Investigations with Sampling
series_description: Enforcement investigations conducted by Public Employment Risk Reduction Program (PERRP) Compliance Safety and Health Officers (CSHO) as a result of public employee/employer complaints, exposure records, fatalities and other case files that involve PERRP industrial hygiene sampling data that requires analysis.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan ; Electronic: Retain for 40 years after final abatement verification is received

series_description: The Public Employment Risk Reduction Program (PERRP)investigations of public employees/employer complaints, multiple hospitalizations, fatalities, refusals-to work, and any other range of case files. This includes occupational health investigations where sampling data was not collected.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after final abatement verification is received

series_description: Inspection records of public employer workplaces that indentify occupational safety and health hazards and alleged violations of adopted Ohio employment risk reduction standards.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years after report is mailed to the employer

series_title: Public Employer (PE) Request for Variances and Undue Hardships
series_description: A Public Employer (PE) unable to comply with an adopted or proposed Public Employment Risk Reduction Program (PERRP) standard may apply for a temporary variance of the standard by providing a justification as to why they are unable to comply and specify what, if any, hardship they will incur in complying with the PERRP standard. The application must include a statement of the steps the PE will take or has taken to comply with Ohio PERRP standards.
retention_statement: Electronic: Remain on file until application has been fulfilled and any extensions have been exhausted. The administrator may renew an order issued up to two times provided that the requirements of the approved variance application are met and the public employer files an application for renewal with the administrator at least ninety days prior to the expiration date of the order

series_description: This schedule relates to PE requests submitted to Public Employment Risk Reduction Program (PERRP) requesting exemption from program standards, requests, inspection reports and approvals/denials of requirements.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 7 years

series_title: Public Employer (PE) Injury, Occupational Illness and Accident Statistical Records
series_description: This schedule relates to the collection and compilation of statistical data submitted by PEs to Public Employment Risk Reduction Program (PERRP) regarding the cause and prevention of injuries, occupational illnesses and accidents to public employees.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain data has been entered into reporting database ; Electronic: Retain data a minimum of 10 years, possibly indefinitely pursuant to adopted OSHA recordkeeping standards.

series_description: Informal and invalid complaints are submitted by public employees (PE), public employee representatives or the general public regarding workplace safety and health concerns. Examples of these complaints would be no jurisdiction of right of entry or required action by employer. Invalid Complaints are issues not applicable to Public Employment Risk Reduction Program (PERRP) standards.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned and is deemed readable ; Electronic: Retain in database for 3 years.

series_description: The Self Insured (SI) Risk Files contain all employer related information pertaining to the policy. Once the SI Risk File has been received it is then scanned into the (UDS) Universal Document Service. The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images. The information included in the SI risk files is the employers initial application for self insurance, assessment information, all risk related correspondence, renewal applications and other related documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan the original document into UDS, verify the scanned image is readable, and ; Electronic: Retain in UDS for 18 years after employer cancels coverage, and

series_title: Enforcement Citations, Internal Investigation Report and Citation Worksheets
series_description: Public Employment Risk Reduction Program (PERRP) Compliance Safety and Health Officer's (CSHO) worksheets of internal reports to support citations issued to employers.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan and ; Electronic: Retain until no longer have administrative value determined by PERRP

series_description: The Qualified Health Plan (QHP) application includes the Self Insured (SI) employer’s information to plan for the medical management of it’s claims. The information in the QHP application includes written information detailing the QHP contract information, organization, health care provider network, medical case and utilization management.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until the file is cancelled or files bankruptcy ; Paper: Retain until the file is cancelled or files bankruptcy, retain for 17 years, and

series_description: Description of duties to be performed and the percentage of time spent on each duty. Each position has an unique number and an unique position description.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan hard copies into UDS and retain until checked for quality assurance ; Electronic: Retain until position description becomes obsolete

series_description: The Payroll Records contain payroll information for BWC employees such as donated leave requests, overtime (OT) forms, off-cycle paychecks, reversal or return of direct deposit requests, manual/additional pays, and miscellaneous emails from employees regarding time keeping changes (i.e., RFL type, comp vs. OT, etc).
retention_statement: Paper: Scan the original document into J: drive (Payroll), verify the scanned image is readable, and ; Electronic: Retain for one (1) year, provided audited by Auditor of State, audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved, and

series_description: The Personnel Files are electronic records of all actions pertaining to each individual BWC employee. The Personnel Files cover all the employees’ records for the duration of employment with BWC. Records contained in the Personnel Files are performance evaluations, commendation letters, position changes, tax information, the Early Retirement Initiative (ERI) letter, approval of retirement from the Ohio Public Employees Retirement System (OPERS), and personal information regarding medical information. This series contains personnel actions and attachments: Personnel Actions (ADM-4100). Documents may include Certificates of Achievement, Licenses, and Degrees, Professional Reference Letters, Copies of Military Discharge Reports (DD-214) and Employee applications of employment. Personnel actions submitted for permanent retention include: Removal Documents, Theft-in-Office Documents (The only criminal records to be sent),New-Hire documents (Employee applications, Supplemental Employment Agreements, Position Descriptions, Teaching certificate copies, Certificates relevant to the job, and other correspondences relative to the position), Updated Applications, State Personnel Board of Review Orders and correspondences, Death Certificates, attached to the appropriate P/A. The following must NOT be sent to DAS Records in accordance with Section 149.43 of the Ohio Revised Code or collective bargaining arbitrations: Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) Information form, Letters of recommendation, Medical records (physician statements, disability paperwork, Criminal records, except for "Theft-in-Office", Disciplinary records, except for Removals. Agencies are instructed to adopt records retention schedules for agency copies of personnel records, including copies of documents that are submitted to DAS and items that are not part of the employee personnel file maintained by DAS. These are for all classified and unclassified state employees.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain scanned material in UDS (or any database that replaces it) for 7 years after the employee’s date of separation from BWC, and

series_description: The Lien Releases are filed in non-complying employer claims and premium balances. These liens are placed once an employer has non-compliant claims filed and the billing is certified to the Attorney General’s office for non-payment and/or an employer’s premium balance has been certified to the AG for non-payment. The employer did not maintain Workers’ Compensation at the time the claim was filed. The liens are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain paper until balance is paid in full and the lien is released – ; Electronic: Review machine readable information every 3 years to ensure that files can still be accessed. Retain in UDS for 15 years after the date of the release

series_description: The Audio/Visual Correspondence is an email generated by the BWC library with the purpose of letting a person or company know when a barrowed audio/visual tape, DVD, or Power Point presentation is past the due date. A correspondence is also generated when the material has been damaged or lost and BWC is requesting the burrower replace the material.
retention_statement: Paper: Keep overdue notice until audio/visual material is returned ; Electronic: Keep overdue notice until audio/visual material is returned

series_title: Long Term Care Loan (LTCL) Program Application
series_description: Employers in the healthcare field seek reimbursement from BWC by submitting the LTCL program application. Ohio nursing homes and hospitals apply for reimbursement of interest paid on loans used to purchase safety equipment to reduce workplace injuries resulting from manually lifting patients
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for a total of 20 years. This includes the 15 years as required by ORC 2305.06 plus the 5 year life of the agreement.

series_description: The Adjudication Committee Hearing Records are created for The Adjudicating Committee Hearings. These records contain the employer’s formal complaint “e.g. protest letters” the Bureau’s position on the issue, and/or the Employer Group’s formal complaint, statement of facts, complaint decision, compliant denial letter, and any cases, rules, or laws being relied upon by the parties. The decision Order drafted by the Committee Chairperson in Legal or the decision Order of the Administrator’s Designee when appealed to a second level hearing. These Orders may be appealed to Court by the Employer or the Employer Group. The Adjudicating Committee Hearing Records are scanned into the Universal Document Service (UDS). The Universal Document Service (UDS) is an imaging concept that stores any type of electronic file, such as Microsoft Office documents, Adobe Acrobat, audio/video, Computer Aided Design (CAD), etc. as well as scanned images.
retention_statement: Electronic: The Adjudication Committee Hearing Records are drafted, kept, and published into a PDF in the Legal Division’s Adjudicating SharePoint site. Scan the final Portable Document Format (PDF) into the Universal Document Service (UDS) Employer Services Policy Risk file and verify for quality assurance. Retain for ten (10) years after the final adjudicating order, and ; Paper: Retain for ten (10) years after the final adjudicating order OR 30 days after being converted to an electronic record, and

series_title: Ohio Occupational Safety & Health Research Program File
series_description: The Research Program files consist of research agreements, applications, reviewer scoring summaries, quarterly, interim and final reports, and other related files. Since files related to this program include the research agreements, or contracts, we need to retain the files in keeping with O.R.C. 2305.06, or for nine years from the date of expiration or termination of the contract.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 9 years from the date of expiration or termination of the contract ; Electronic: Retain 9 years from the date of expiration or termination of the contract

series_description: Copies of claim information and correspondence a DEP Providers would use to provide evaluation services to the BWC. These records are recovered from a DEP, a DEP Administrative Agent, or other DEP subcontractor.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 3 years, and ; Paper: Retain 3 years, and

series_description: The bankruptcy files are BWC’s record of the proceedings of bankruptcy cases. The bankruptcy files contain correspondence and pleadings in bankruptcy actions in which the BWC is a party.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan the original document into UDS, verify the scanned image is readable, and ; Electronic: The bankruptcy files are scanned into Universal Document Service (UDS). Retain the electronic version of the bankruptcy files in UDS for 30 years after the bankruptcy case closes. The electronic version information will be reviewed every 3 years to ensure that the files can still be accessed.

series_description: The lost time claim file contains information pertaining to a claimant’s claim for workers’ compensation benefits, such as, but not limited to: medical records, requests for treatment, requests for payment of compensation, compensation payment records, and orders issued by BWC or the Industrial Commission. A lost time claim is a claim in which compensation benefits have been paid, such as, but not limited to: temporary total disability compensation, salary continuation, permanent partial disability compensation, or permanent total disability compensation. The statute of limitations for lost time claims is as follows: •For claims with DOI prior to 8/25/06, the claim expires ten (10) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, or date of death. •For claims with DOI from 8/25/06 to 9/14/20, the claim expires five (5) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, or date of death. •For claims with DOI on or after 9/15/2020, the claim expires five (5) years from the date of the last medical service rendered, compensation payment, or date of death.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain the lost time claim file for 6 years after the claim has reached the appropriate statute of limitations (i.e. expiration date) according to the date of injury (DOI) year. Digitally image claim file records, retain for 30 days for quality control, and ; Electronic: Retain the lost time claim file for 6 years after the claim has reached the appropriate statute of limitations (i.e. expiration date) according to the date of injury (DOI) year. ; Microform: Retain the lost time claim file for 6 years after the claim has reached the appropriate statute of limitations (i.e. expiration date) according to the date of injury (DOI) year.
